description.content: Run analytical queries against Keen event collections.
Returns the number of events in a collection that match the supplied parameters.

query.description: (Required) Name of the event collection to query.
(Required) Relative or absolute time frame for the query (e.g., this_7_days).
JSON-encoded array of filter objects to narrow events.
Property name to group results by.
Time-based grouping interval (e.g., daily, hourly).
Time zone offset for relative timeframes.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Analyzes user progression through an ordered sequence of steps, each defined as a query against an event collection.

response.name: Funnel result with counts per step.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Runs multiple analyses on the same event collection in a single request, returning all results in a combined response.
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

The Keen Query API exposes the analytical query types provided by the Keen analytics platform: count, count_unique, sum, average, minimum, maximum, median, percentile, select_unique, funnel, and multi_analysis. Queries support filters, time frames, intervals, group-by, and time zone parameters for flexible multi-dimensional analysis of event data stored in Keen event collections.
